items.title: Finalize ARM64 Buildroot kernel configuration
items.description: Complete the Buildroot defconfig with all required packages for minimal bootable system including networking, filesystem, init system, cgroups, namespaces, and overlayfs for container support.
acceptance_criteria: Buildroot config produces bootable ARM64 image
Networking stack functional (bridge, veth, iptables for containers)
Init system (OpenRC) launches containerd on boot
cgroups v2 and namespaces enabled for container isolation
overlayfs enabled for container storage driver
Root filesystem type defined (ext4 or squashfs)
Boot loader configured (U-Boot for SBC targets)

items.title: Set up containerd runtime with boot integration
items.description: Install and configure containerd as the container runtime on the ARM64 base system. Ensure it starts on boot via OpenRC and Docker CLI can interact with it.
acceptance_criteria: Containerd installed and starts on boot via OpenRC service
Docker CLI can interact with containerd (pull, run, stop)
Container networking functional (bridge mode)
Containerd survives restart without data loss
Storage driver uses overlayfs on ext4 partition

items.title: Configure GPU/NPU device passthrough for containers
items.description: Enable hardware acceleration passthrough into containers. Target Tier 1 hardware: Jetson Orin (CUDA), RK3588 (RKNN), Pi 5 + Hailo-10H. Include CPU-only fallback for unsupported hardware.
acceptance_criteria: NPU/GPU devices visible inside containers via device passthrough
Jetson Orin: CUDA GPU accessible, Ollama uses GPU acceleration
RK3588: RKNN NPU accessible via /dev/rknpu, RKLLM toolkit functional
Pi 5 + Hailo-10H: Hailo device accessible via HailoRT
CPU-only fallback when no accelerator present (llama.cpp NEON/SVE)
Performance benchmark per target: tok/s for Qwen2.5-Coder-7B Q4

items.title: Deploy Ollama + ChromaDB AI stack via Docker Compose
items.description: Validate and harden the Docker Compose configuration for Ollama LLM server and ChromaDB vector database. Resolve LanceDB/ChromaDB mismatch in bmt_ai_os-build/. Ensure data persistence and service resilience.
acceptance_criteria: Ollama serves model inference on port 11434
ChromaDB accepts vector operations on port 8000
Services auto-restart on failure (restart policy configured)
Model data persisted via named volume (survives container restart)
ChromaDB data persisted via named volume (survives container restart)
Resource limits set (CPU, memory) appropriate per device tier
bmt_ai_os-build/services/ai-stack.yml updated to ChromaDB (remove LanceDB reference)

items.title: Implement BMT AI OS controller for AI stack orchestration
items.description: Extend the controller prototype to manage the lifecycle of AI stack containers (Ollama, ChromaDB, and future AI services). Scope is limited to AI-stack services, not system-wide container management.
acceptance_criteria: Controller starts/stops AI stack containers (Ollama, ChromaDB) on demand
Health check polling with configurable interval (default 30s)
Auto-restart failed AI stack containers
Controller exposes status endpoint or CLI showing service health
Scope limited to AI-stack services defined in docker-compose.yml
Graceful shutdown handling (SIGTERM)

items.title: Build document ingestion pipeline into ChromaDB
items.description: Create an ingestion pipeline that reads documents, chunks them, generates embeddings via Qwen3-Embedding, and stores them in ChromaDB for retrieval.
acceptance_criteria: Supports ingestion of plain text (.txt), Markdown (.md), and source code files
Documents chunked with configurable chunk size (default 512 tokens)
Embeddings generated via Ollama embedding model (default: Qwen3-Embedding or nomic-embed-text)
Chunks stored in ChromaDB with source metadata (filename, position, language)
Ingestion of 100 documents completes within 60 seconds on Jetson Orin

items.title: Build RAG query and augmented generation pipeline
items.description: Implement the retrieval-augmented generation flow: accept a user query, retrieve relevant chunks from ChromaDB, and pass context to Ollama for augmented response generation.
acceptance_criteria: User query retrieves top-k relevant chunks from ChromaDB (k configurable, default 5)
Retrieved context injected into prompt template
LLM generates augmented response using retrieved context
End-to-end query latency under 3 seconds on Jetson Orin with Qwen2.5-Coder-7B Q4
Response includes source attribution (which documents were used)

items.title: Design and implement LLM provider abstraction layer
items.description: Create a unified provider interface in the controller that abstracts LLM backends behind a common API. Support provider registration, configuration, and runtime switching. Local providers are primary; cloud providers are optional fallbacks.
acceptance_criteria: Provider interface defines chat(), embed(), and list_models() methods
Provider registry allows registering and selecting backends by name
Configuration file (YAML) specifies active provider and fallback chain
Ollama provider implemented as reference (including Anthropic-compatible API for Claude Code)
Provider can be switched at runtime without restarting the controller

items.title: Implement local LLM provider: vLLM
items.description: Add vLLM as a local inference backend. Higher throughput than Ollama for batch workloads. OpenAI-compatible API out of the box.
acceptance_criteria: vLLM service added to docker-compose.yml with ARM64-compatible image
Provider implements chat() and list_models() via vLLM OpenAI-compatible API
Provider implements embed() or falls back to Ollama for embeddings
Model loading and switching works via provider config
Health check integration with controller

items.title: Implement local LLM provider: llama.cpp server
items.description: Add llama.cpp (via llama-server) as a lightweight local inference backend. Best option for resource-constrained ARM64 devices. Native NEON/SVE and KleidiAI optimizations for ARM.
acceptance_criteria: llama-server service added to docker-compose.yml (or binary install)
Provider implements chat() and embed() via llama.cpp HTTP API
GGUF model format support (Q4_K_M default quantization)
Memory footprint documented vs Ollama on same model
ARM NEON/SVE acceleration verified, RKNN delegation when available

items.title: Implement cloud LLM provider: OpenAI
items.description: Add OpenAI as a cloud provider backend. Also serves as base class for OpenAI-compatible providers (Groq, Mistral).
acceptance_criteria: Provider implements chat() via OpenAI Chat Completions API
Provider implements embed() via OpenAI Embeddings API
API key configured via environment variable or secrets file (never hardcoded)
Graceful error handling when offline or rate-limited
Request/response logging for cost tracking

items.title: Implement cloud LLM provider: Anthropic (Claude)
items.description: Add Anthropic Claude as a cloud provider backend. Supports tool use and extended thinking.
acceptance_criteria: Provider implements chat() via Anthropic Messages API
Handles Anthropic-specific features (system prompt, tool use)
API key configured via environment variable or secrets file
Graceful error handling when offline or rate-limited
Request/response logging for cost tracking

items.title: Implement cloud LLM provider: Google Gemini
items.description: Add Google Gemini as a cloud provider backend.
acceptance_criteria: Provider implements chat() via Gemini API
Provider implements embed() via Gemini Embedding API
API key configured via environment variable or secrets file
Graceful error handling when offline or rate-limited
Request/response logging for cost tracking

items.title: Implement cloud LLM providers: Mistral and Groq
items.description: Add Mistral and Groq as cloud provider backends. Both use OpenAI-compatible APIs, reuse OpenAI base class.
acceptance_criteria: Mistral provider implements chat() and embed() via Mistral API
Groq provider implements chat() via Groq API (OpenAI-compatible)
Shared OpenAI-compatible base class reused for both
API keys configured independently per provider
Groq noted as low-latency cloud fallback option

items.title: Implement provider fallback chain and routing logic
items.description: Build the routing layer that tries providers in priority order (local-first, cloud fallback). Handle failures gracefully with configurable retry and timeout per provider.
acceptance_criteria: Fallback chain defined in config (e.g., ollama → llama.cpp → openai → anthropic)
Automatic failover to next provider on timeout or error
Configurable timeout per provider (default 30s local, 15s cloud)
Circuit breaker: skip unhealthy providers for configurable cooldown period
Logs which provider served each request

items.title: Package and pre-install AI coding CLIs (Claude Code, Aider, Continue, Tabby)
items.description: Bundle the top AI coding CLI tools into the BMT AI OS image. Includes Tabby as self-hosted Copilot alternative (32k GitHub stars, Rust-based, supports fine-tuning on private repos).
acceptance_criteria: Claude Code installed and functional (uses Ollama Anthropic-compatible API since v0.14)
Aider installed and functional (pip package)
Continue.dev installed as CLI/server component
Tabby server installed (self-hosted code completion)
Open Interpreter installed and functional
All CLIs launch without errors on ARM64
Version pinning for reproducible installs

items.title: Auto-configure coding CLIs to use local LLM providers
items.description: Generate default configuration for each coding CLI pointing to local providers. Use Ollama Anthropic-compatible API for Claude Code, OpenAI-compatible API for Aider/Continue/Tabby.
acceptance_criteria: Claude Code configured via ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL=http://localhost:11434
Aider defaults to local Ollama model (Qwen2.5-Coder-7B or Qwen3.5-9B)
Continue config.json auto-generated with Ollama provider (Qwen3 30B-A3B recommended)
Tabby configured with local model backend
Open Interpreter configured to use local LLM endpoint
Cloud API keys auto-detected from env vars when present
Config regenerated when provider settings change

items.title: Add IDE AI plugin support (Cursor, Copilot, Cody)
items.description: Expose OpenAI-compatible API endpoint so remote IDEs can use BMT AI OS as their AI backend. Streaming SSE support for real-time completions.
acceptance_criteria: OpenAI-compatible API served on configurable port (default 8080)
Cursor can connect using custom API base URL pointing to BMT AI OS
Copilot-compatible endpoint for code completions
Cody compatible endpoint for chat and embeddings
API supports streaming responses (SSE) for real-time completions
Connection guide documented for each IDE

items.title: Add code agent support (SWE-agent, Codex CLI, Mentat)
items.description: Package autonomous code agents that can operate on local repositories using the local LLM stack. Sandboxed execution in containers with mounted workspaces.
acceptance_criteria: SWE-agent installed and runnable against local repos
Codex CLI installed with --oss flag for local Ollama backend
Mentat installed with local provider configuration
Agents can access local filesystem and git repos
Sandboxed execution: agents run in containers with mounted workspaces
Agent output logged for review

items.title: Implement workspace and project context management for coding tools
items.description: Shared workspace mounting and codebase indexing into ChromaDB so all coding CLIs and agents leverage RAG-enhanced code understanding. Use Qwen3-Embedding for code-optimized embeddings.
acceptance_criteria: Project directories mountable into tool containers via controller CLI
Codebase auto-indexed into ChromaDB on mount (using BMTOS-5a ingestion)
Indexed context available to all coding CLIs via RAG pipeline
File watcher re-indexes changed files incrementally
Multiple workspaces supported simultaneously
Workspace context isolated per project in ChromaDB collections

items.title: Create coding model preset manager
items.description: Manage recommended model presets optimized for coding tasks. Auto-detect device RAM/NPU and recommend optimal preset. Ship Qwen-family models as defaults (best open-source coding models in 2026).
acceptance_criteria: Preset 'lite': Qwen3.5-9B Q4 (~6GB RAM) for Pi 5 / RK3588 8GB
Preset 'standard': Qwen2.5-Coder-7B Q4 + Qwen3-Embedding-8B (~8GB) for RK3588 16GB / Jetson
Preset 'full': Qwen3.5-27B Q4 + Qwen3-Embedding-8B (~18GB) for Jetson / RK3588 32GB
Auto-detect device RAM/NPU and recommend preset
One-command model pull: bmt_ai_os models install coding-standard
Preset config shared across all coding CLIs

items.title: Build bootable ARM64 image pipeline
items.description: Create the end-to-end build pipeline that takes Buildroot config + BitBake layers from bmt_ai_os-build/ and produces a flashable ARM64 image. Support multiple board targets.
acceptance_criteria: Build script produces bootable .img file for at least one Tier 1 target
Integrates bmt_ai_os-build/ BitBake layers (hardware-accel.bb, container-engine.bb)
Image boots on QEMU ARM64 emulator (qemu-system-aarch64)
Image boots on target hardware (Jetson Orin or RK3588)
Build reproducible with pinned package versions
Build artifacts documented (sizes, partitions, bootloader)

items.title: Configure init system and service boot ordering
items.description: Define the full boot sequence: network → storage → containerd → AI stack → controller. Handle first-boot initialization (volume creation, initial model pull). Use OpenRC as init system per base-config.toml.
acceptance_criteria: OpenRC service files for containerd, ollama, chromadb, controller
Service dependencies enforced (network before containerd, containerd before AI stack)
First-boot script initializes volumes, pulls default model preset
Boot-to-ready time measured and documented per target
System recovers gracefully from unclean shutdown
15+ consecutive boot cycles without failure

items.title: Set up QEMU testing and CI pipeline
items.description: Automated testing pipeline that builds the image, boots it in QEMU ARM64, and runs integration tests verifying all services come up. Essential for development without physical hardware.
acceptance_criteria: QEMU ARM64 setup scripted and documented
CI pipeline builds image and boots in QEMU on every commit
Integration tests verify: containerd running, Ollama responding, ChromaDB responding
Controller health endpoint returns healthy status
Test results logged and accessible
GitHub Actions workflow (or equivalent) committed

items.title: Design filesystem layout and storage partitioning
items.description: Define the root filesystem layout, data partition for models/vectors, and persistent storage strategy. Models (5-20GB) and vector DB data need dedicated persistent storage that survives OS updates.
acceptance_criteria: Root filesystem layout documented (/etc, /var, /opt/bmt_ai_os)
Dedicated data partition for AI models and ChromaDB volumes
Partition scheme supports A/B root for future OTA updates
Model storage location standardized (/var/lib/ollama/models)
ChromaDB data at /var/lib/chromadb with backup strategy
Disk usage documented per preset (lite ~10GB, standard ~15GB, full ~25GB)

items.title: Implement service discovery and container networking
items.description: Configure internal DNS and networking so containers resolve each other by hostname. External services accessible via host ports. Isolated container network for AI stack.
acceptance_criteria: Containers resolve each other by service name (ollama, chromadb)
Docker Compose network isolates AI stack from host network
Host can reach services via localhost ports (11434, 8000)
DNS resolution works inside containers for external lookups
Network survives container restarts without stale entries

items.title: Configure container security and secrets management
items.description: Harden container security: non-root execution, read-only filesystems where possible, capability dropping. Implement secrets management for cloud API keys.
acceptance_criteria: AI stack containers run as non-root user where possible
Secrets stored in /etc/bmt_ai_os/secrets/ with restricted permissions (0600)
Cloud API keys loaded from secrets files, never from environment in logs
Container capabilities dropped to minimum required set
No hardcoded credentials in any image layer
Security policy documented

items.title: Build BMT AI OS CLI tool (bmt_ai_os command)
items._epic_note: Standalone — cross-cutting CLI used by all epics
items.description: Create the primary user-facing CLI for interacting with BMT AI OS: manage models, query RAG, check status, configure providers. Single entry point for all OS AI capabilities.
acceptance_criteria: bmt_ai_os status — show system health, active provider, loaded model
bmt_ai_os models list/install/remove — model management via Ollama
bmt_ai_os query 'question' — RAG query against indexed workspace
bmt_ai_os ingest /path/to/docs — index documents into ChromaDB
bmt_ai_os provider set ollama|vllm|openai — switch active provider
bmt_ai_os workspace add /path — mount and index a project
Help text and --help for all subcommands

items.title: Expose external REST API for AI stack access
items.description: HTTP REST API for external apps to query the RAG pipeline, manage models, and interact with the AI stack. OpenAI-compatible endpoint for drop-in integration with any tool.
acceptance_criteria: POST /api/v1/chat — chat completion (OpenAI-compatible format)
POST /api/v1/embeddings — generate embeddings
POST /api/v1/query — RAG-augmented query with source attribution
GET /api/v1/models — list available models
GET /api/v1/health — system health check
Streaming responses via SSE for chat completions
API key authentication (optional, configurable)

items.title: Build centralized logging and system metrics
items.description: Aggregate logs from all services (controller, Ollama, ChromaDB, containers). Collect system metrics (CPU, RAM, temperature, inference tok/s). Essential for debugging and performance tuning on edge hardware.
acceptance_criteria: All container logs aggregated to syslog or journal
System metrics collected: CPU, RAM, disk, temperature
Inference metrics: tokens/sec, model load time, queue depth
bmt_ai_os logs — tail aggregated logs
bmt_ai_os metrics — show current system stats
Log retention policy: rotate at 100MB, keep 7 days

items.title: Implement OS update mechanism with rollback
items.description: Update the OS kernel, system packages, and container images with rollback capability. A/B partition scheme for safe OS updates. Container image updates independent of OS updates.
acceptance_criteria: OS update fetches new rootfs and writes to inactive partition
Bootloader switches to new partition on next boot
Automatic rollback if new partition fails health check
Container image updates independent (docker compose pull)
Model data and ChromaDB data preserved across updates
bmt_ai_os update — single command for full system update

items.title: Add Jetson Orin board support package
items.description: Specific BSP for NVIDIA Jetson Orin Nano Super: CUDA toolkit, TensorRT-LLM, JetPack components integration. Primary Tier 1 target — 67 TOPS, 7B models at 15-22 tok/s.
acceptance_criteria: Jetson Orin Nano Super boots BMT AI OS image
CUDA GPU accessible inside containers
Ollama uses CUDA acceleration for inference
Qwen2.5-Coder-7B Q4 achieves 15+ tok/s
TensorRT-LLM available as optional acceleration path
Thermal management: throttle inference on overtemp

items.title: Add Rockchip RK3588 board support package
items.description: BSP for RK3588 boards (Orange Pi 5, ROCK 5B): RKNN NPU driver, RKLLM toolkit integration. Best value Tier 1 target ($100-180) but worst software fragmentation.
acceptance_criteria: Orange Pi 5 or ROCK 5B boots BMT AI OS image
RKNN NPU accessible (/dev/rknpu) inside containers
RKLLM toolkit runs TinyLlama 1.1B at 15+ tok/s on NPU
CPU-only path runs Qwen3.5-9B Q4 via llama.cpp (NEON)
Board-specific device tree and U-Boot config
8GB and 16GB RAM variants tested

items.title: Add Raspberry Pi 5 + Hailo AI HAT+ 2 board support package
items.description: BSP for Pi 5 with Hailo-10H accelerator (40 TOPS, 8GB onboard RAM). Supports models up to 1.5B params at ~9.5 tok/s. Largest community of any ARM64 SBC.
acceptance_criteria: Pi 5 + AI HAT+ 2 boots BMT AI OS image
Hailo-10H device accessible via HailoRT inside containers
Qwen2.5-1.5B runs at ~9 tok/s on Hailo NPU
CPU-only fallback runs Qwen3.5-9B Q4 at ~4-6 tok/s
Power consumption documented (target <15W during inference)
8GB Pi 5 variant tested

items.title: Scaffold Next.js dashboard app with shadcn/ui
items.description: Initialize the dashboard as a Next.js App Router project with shadcn/ui, Tailwind CSS, and TypeScript. Set up project structure, theme provider (dark default), layout shell with sidebar navigation, and API route handlers that proxy to the controller backend.
acceptance_criteria: Next.js 15+ app created at bmt_ai_os/dashboard/ with App Router
shadcn/ui initialized with 'new-york' style, dark theme default
Tailwind CSS configured with shadcn/ui CSS variables
Layout shell: sidebar nav (shadcn Sidebar), top bar, main content area
Nav items: Overview, Models, RAG Console, Providers, Logs, Coding Tools
Theme toggle (dark/light) using shadcn/ui ThemeProvider (next-themes)
Dashboard served on port 9090 (standard admin/monitoring port)
API route handlers at /api/ proxying to controller backend
WebSocket hook (useWebSocket) for real-time data streaming
Docker build stage: static export or standalone for ARM64 deployment
Production build under 2MB gzipped

items.title: Build system overview dashboard page
items.description: Main dashboard landing page showing system health at a glance. Uses shadcn/ui Card, Badge, Progress components for service status, system gauges, and inference metrics. Real-time updates via WebSocket.
acceptance_criteria: Service health Cards: Badge (green/yellow/red) for Ollama, ChromaDB, Controller
System gauges: Progress bars for CPU %, RAM %, disk %, temperature
Active model Card with size, quantization, and provider info
Inference metrics: tok/s sparkline (recharts), avg latency, total requests
Uptime counter per service
Real-time updates via WebSocket (no polling)
Responsive grid layout: mobile (1 col) and desktop (2-3 col)
Uses shadcn/ui: Card, Badge, Progress, Separator, Skeleton (loading states)

items.title: Build model manager dashboard page
items.description: Web UI for browsing, downloading, switching, and removing LLM models. Uses shadcn/ui Table, Dialog, Button, Progress for model library, install actions, and disk usage. Streaming download progress via WebSocket.
acceptance_criteria: Installed models Table: name, size, quantization, last used, disk usage
Available models: Command palette (shadcn Command) to search Ollama library
One-click download with Progress bar (streaming via WebSocket)
Delete model via AlertDialog confirmation
Switch active model via Select dropdown, no page reload
Preset quick-install Buttons: lite, standard, full
Disk usage summary Card: total storage used vs available
Uses shadcn/ui: Table, Command, Dialog, AlertDialog, Button, Progress, Select

items.title: Build RAG console dashboard page
items.description: Web interface for document ingestion and RAG queries. Uses shadcn/ui for chat-style query interface with streaming responses, file upload dropzone, and collapsible source attribution panels.
acceptance_criteria: Document upload: drag-and-drop zone using Input file + custom dropzone
Workspace browser: Tabs per collection, Table of indexed docs with count
Chat-style query interface: Input + Button, streaming response via SSE
Source attribution: Collapsible panels showing matched chunks per response
Chunk preview: Accordion with highlighted matching text
Ingestion progress: Toast notifications for chunking/embedding/storage status
Collection management: create (Dialog), delete (AlertDialog), re-index (Button)
Uses shadcn/ui: Tabs, Table, Input, Button, Accordion, Collapsible, Toast, Dialog

items.title: Build provider configuration dashboard page
items.description: Web UI for configuring LLM providers. Uses shadcn/ui Switch toggles, sortable fallback chain with drag-and-drop, masked Input fields for API keys, and per-provider health Badges.
acceptance_criteria: Provider list: Cards with Switch toggle (enable/disable) per provider
Fallback chain: sortable list (dnd-kit) for drag-and-drop reordering
API key management: masked Input fields with eye toggle, save to secrets
Per-provider stats Card: requests served, avg latency, error rate
Health Badge per provider (green/yellow/red)
Test connection Button with loading Spinner and Toast result
Changes apply without controller restart (optimistic UI update)
Uses shadcn/ui: Card, Switch, Input, Button, Badge, Toast, Separator

items.title: Build logs viewer dashboard page
items.description: Real-time log viewer using shadcn/ui. Virtualized log list for performance, filter toggles per service, log level badges, and monospace ScrollArea. WebSocket streaming for live-tail.
acceptance_criteria: Live-tail logs via WebSocket in virtualized ScrollArea (react-virtual)
Filter ToggleGroup buttons per service (controller, Ollama, ChromaDB)
Log level filter: ToggleGroup for debug/info/warn/error
Text search: Input with real-time filtering
Auto-scroll with pause-on-scroll-up, resume Button at bottom
Log lines: monospace font, color-coded Badge per level, timestamp
Download logs Button exports visible logs as .txt
Uses shadcn/ui: ScrollArea, ToggleGroup, Input, Button, Badge

items.title: Build coding tools status dashboard page
items.description: Dashboard page showing installed coding CLIs and agents using shadcn/ui Cards with status Badges, workspace Table, and IDE connection info with copy-to-clipboard.
acceptance_criteria: Tool Cards: Claude Code, Aider, Continue, Tabby with version and Badge status
Config Badge: green (local provider), yellow (cloud), red (not configured)
Workspaces Table: mounted projects, indexed doc counts, last re-index time
Quick action Buttons: re-index workspace, reconfigure tool
IDE connection Card: API endpoint URL with copy-to-clipboard for Cursor/Copilot/Cody
External links to each tool's documentation
Uses shadcn/ui: Card, Badge, Table, Button, Tooltip

items.title: Build terminal UI (TUI) dashboard with Textual
items.description: Terminal-based dashboard using Python Textual framework. Provides the same system monitoring and management as the web dashboard but directly in the terminal — no browser needed. Essential for headless servers and SSH sessions.
acceptance_criteria: TUI launches via 'bmt_ai_os tui' command
System overview tab: service health, CPU/RAM/disk/temp gauges, tok/s
Models tab: list installed models, pull/remove with progress bar
Logs tab: live-tail with service filter and log level filter
Providers tab: list providers with status, switch active provider
RAG tab: query input with streaming response display
Keyboard navigation: tab switching, vim-style scrolling
Runs over SSH without X11 forwarding
Built with Python Textual (matches controller language)
Color theme matches web dashboard dark mode

items.title: Install PyTorch and ML training framework for ARM64
items.description: Package PyTorch (ARM64-native), Hugging Face Transformers, and core ML libraries into the OS image. Enable on-device model training and fine-tuning. PyTorch must use ARM NEON/SVE optimizations and CUDA when available on Jetson.
acceptance_criteria: PyTorch installed with ARM64 NEON/SVE optimizations (pip install torch)
Jetson target: PyTorch uses CUDA GPU acceleration
RK3588/Pi targets: PyTorch runs CPU-only with NEON vectorization
Hugging Face Transformers library installed
Hugging Face Datasets library installed
torch.cuda.is_available() returns True on Jetson, False on others
Basic training smoke test: fine-tune a 100-param model on dummy data

items.title: Implement LoRA/QLoRA fine-tuning pipeline
items.description: Enable lightweight fine-tuning of small LLMs (1-3B params) on edge hardware using LoRA and QLoRA. Integrate PEFT library with Hugging Face Trainer. Target: fine-tune a 1.5B model on Jetson Orin with 8GB RAM.
acceptance_criteria: PEFT library installed with LoRA and QLoRA support
bitsandbytes installed for 4-bit quantization (QLoRA)
Fine-tune Qwen2.5-1.5B with LoRA on custom dataset (Jetson: <4GB VRAM)
Fine-tune with QLoRA on same model (<2GB VRAM for 4-bit base)
Training produces adapter weights saved to /var/lib/bmt_ai_os/adapters/
Adapter weights loadable by Ollama via Modelfile
Training script: bmt_ai_os train --model qwen2.5:1.5b --data /path/to/data
Training resumes from checkpoint on interruption

items.title: Build training data preparation tools
items.description: CLI tools for preparing training datasets from local documents, code, and conversations. Convert raw data into instruction-tuning format (Alpaca, ShareGPT, ChatML). Validate and preview datasets before training.
acceptance_criteria: bmt_ai_os data prepare --input /docs --format alpaca — convert docs to training format
Supported input: .txt, .md, .jsonl, .csv, code files
Output formats: Alpaca (instruction/input/output), ShareGPT (conversations), ChatML
Auto-generate instruction/response pairs from documents using local LLM
bmt_ai_os data validate /path/to/dataset.jsonl — check format, stats, issues
bmt_ai_os data preview /path/to/dataset.jsonl — show sample entries
Dataset splitting: auto train/eval split (default 90/10)

items.title: Add Jupyter Notebook server for interactive training
items.description: Pre-installed Jupyter Notebook/Lab accessible via browser for interactive model training, experimentation, and data exploration. Auto-configured with PyTorch and HF libraries.
acceptance_criteria: JupyterLab installed and launchable via bmt_ai_os jupyter
Served on port 8888, accessible from browser
Pre-configured kernel with PyTorch, Transformers, PEFT, Datasets
Example notebooks: LoRA fine-tuning, data preparation, model evaluation
Token authentication enabled by default
Auto-detected GPU/NPU shown in notebook environment info
Notebooks saved to /var/lib/bmt_ai_os/notebooks/

items.title: Implement training monitoring and TensorBoard integration
items.description: Training progress monitoring with loss curves, learning rate, and GPU/NPU utilization. TensorBoard for visualization. Integration with dashboard for training job status.
acceptance_criteria: TensorBoard installed, launchable via bmt_ai_os tensorboard
Served on port 6006, accessible from browser
Training logs written to /var/lib/bmt_ai_os/runs/ in TensorBoard format
Metrics tracked: loss, eval loss, learning rate, GPU memory, tok/s
Dashboard integration: training job Card on system overview page
Training job status: idle/running/complete/failed with progress %
Estimated time remaining based on current throughput

items.title: Build model export and deployment pipeline
items.description: Export fine-tuned models from training into formats deployable by the inference stack. Merge LoRA adapters into base model, convert to GGUF for llama.cpp, or create Ollama Modelfile for direct serving.
acceptance_criteria: bmt_ai_os model merge --base qwen2.5:1.5b --adapter /path/to/lora — merge LoRA weights
bmt_ai_os model export --format gguf — convert merged model to GGUF (Q4_K_M default)
bmt_ai_os model export --format ollama — create Ollama Modelfile and register model
Exported model immediately available in Ollama (ollama run my-fine-tuned-model)
Export preserves tokenizer and chat template
Validation: run inference on 10 test prompts, compare base vs fine-tuned

items.title: Add Apple Silicon board support package (Asahi Linux, CPU-only)
items.description: BSP for Apple Silicon Macs (M1-M4) via Asahi Linux. CPU-only — no GPU acceleration on Linux (Apple GPU driver incomplete). ARM64 CPU with NEON is exceptionally fast for LLM inference via llama.cpp. Highest single-core performance of any Tier 1 target. Unified memory enables large models that don't fit on other boards.
acceptance_criteria: BMT AI OS boots on Apple Silicon via Asahi Linux (M1/M2/M3/M4)
CPU-only inference via llama.cpp with ARM NEON optimization
Qwen2.5-Coder-7B Q4 achieves 30-50 tok/s (CPU-only, M1 baseline)
16GB+ unified RAM enables 13B+ models (not possible on other Tier 1 boards)
Ollama runs natively, no GPU passthrough needed
containerd and Docker CLI functional on Asahi Linux
All coding CLIs and dashboard operational
Known limitation documented: no Metal/GPU acceleration on Linux

items.title: Implement OTA update engine with A/B slot switching
Add multi-user authentication and RBAC to controller API
Build fleet management agent and central registry
Build performance benchmarking suite with CI integration
Implement plugin and extension system
Add Prometheus metrics export and alerting integration
Harden container security with per-service AppArmor/seccomp
Build documentation site with MkDocs and API reference
Add TLS termination and network hardening
Implement structured JSON logging and rotation
Enforce JWT secret and admin password at startup
items.description: JWT secret read from env var but only validated at first token creation. Default admin/admin credentials are a critical security risk. Require BMT_JWT_SECRET (>=32 chars) and BMT_ADMIN_PASS at startup or fail.
acceptance_criteria: Controller refuses to start if BMT_JWT_SECRET is missing or < 32 chars
Controller refuses to start if default admin/admin credentials are active
Clear error message printed to stderr on startup failure

items.title: Replace bare except Exception clauses in API layer
items.description: 7 instances in openai_compat.py and 1 in rag_routes.py swallow errors without context. Replace with typed exceptions, preserve stack traces, sanitize error details for client responses.
acceptance_criteria: Zero bare except Exception clauses in controller/ directory
All exceptions logged with logger.exception() for stack trace
Client-facing error details sanitized (no internal paths/tracebacks)
Request ID included in error log entries

items.title: Add rate limiting to auth and inference endpoints
items.description: No rate limiting on /api/v1/auth/login or /v1/chat/completions. Add per-IP rate limiting using slowapi or custom middleware to prevent brute-force and resource exhaustion.
acceptance_criteria: Login endpoint limited to 5 attempts per 5 minutes per IP
Inference endpoints limited to configurable rate (default 60/min)
Rate limit headers (X-RateLimit-*) included in responses
429 Too Many Requests returned when exceeded

items.title: Add path traversal protection to RAG ingest endpoint
items.description: RAG ingest endpoint accepts arbitrary paths without validation. ingest_directory() uses Path.rglob() without checking if path is within allowed directories. Add whitelist validation.
acceptance_criteria: Configurable allowed_ingest_dirs whitelist
Path.resolve() used to prevent symlink traversal
Requests for paths outside whitelist return 403
Collection name validated with regex: ^[a-zA-Z0-9_-]{1,100}$

items.title: Move secrets from env vars to /run/secrets/ files
items.description: JWT secret and API key read from os.environ, visible in docker inspect and process listings. Refactor to read from /run/secrets/ files with env var fallback for dev mode.
acceptance_criteria: JWT secret read from /run/secrets/BMT_JWT_SECRET first, then env var
API key read from /run/secrets/BMT_API_KEY first, then env var
Docker Compose updated to mount secrets from Docker secrets
Warning logged when falling back to env var in production

items.title: Add all test categories to CI pipeline
items.description: Only smoke tests run in CI. Unit, E2E, load, and security tests are not automated. Add them to the GitHub Actions workflow.
acceptance_criteria: Unit tests (857) run on every push and PR
E2E tests (28) run on every push and PR
Security tests (31) run on every push and PR
Load tests (12) run on main branch pushes only
Test results uploaded as artifacts

items.title: Add token revocation and account lockout
items.description: No way to revoke JWT tokens early or lock accounts after failed logins. Add token blacklist and account lockout mechanism.
acceptance_criteria: Token blacklist table in SQLite; checked during verification
Tokens revoked on user deletion and role change
Account locked after 10 failed login attempts for 15 minutes
Admin can manually lock/unlock accounts

items.title: Add fleet registry persistence with SQLite
items.description: Fleet registry is in-memory only — lost on controller restart. Add SQLite persistence so devices and pending commands survive restarts.
acceptance_criteria: Device records persisted to SQLite on registration and heartbeat
Pending commands persisted and survive controller restart
Registry loads from SQLite on startup
In-memory cache used for hot path (heartbeats)

items.title: Add container vulnerability scanning to CI (trivy)
items.description: No container image scanning or SBOM generation in the build pipeline. Add trivy scanning and CycloneDX SBOM generation.
acceptance_criteria: Trivy scans Docker image on every PR
Critical/High vulnerabilities fail the CI build
SBOM (CycloneDX) generated and uploaded as release artifact
Base image digest pinned in Dockerfile

items.title: Fix CI continue-on-error flags for integration and build jobs
items.description: Integration tests and image build jobs are marked continue-on-error: true. These should be hard gates once QEMU infra is stable.
acceptance_criteria: Integration test job fails the pipeline on failure
Build image job fails the pipeline on failure
ShellCheck errors are non-fatal warnings (keep || true)

items.title: Add password complexity requirements
items.description: No validation on password strength. Users can set single-character passwords. Add minimum length and complexity rules.
acceptance_criteria: Minimum password length: 12 characters
Must contain uppercase, lowercase, and digit
Validation on user creation and password change
Clear error message on validation failure

items.title: Add unit tests for untested core modules
items.description: ~20 core modules have no direct tests: ollama provider, registry, health checker, RAG pipeline (6 modules), fleet agent/collector, CLI. Add targeted unit tests.
acceptance_criteria: Tests for providers/ollama.py, providers/registry.py, providers/config.py
Tests for controller/health.py, controller/config.py
Tests for rag/ingest.py, rag/query.py, rag/storage.py
Tests for fleet/agent.py (offline queue), fleet/collector.py
Total test count >= 1100

items.title: Write production deployment runbook
items.description: No operational documentation for production deployment. Write a runbook covering first-boot setup, monitoring, backups, OTA updates, and troubleshooting.
acceptance_criteria: First-boot checklist (secrets, TLS, admin user)
Monitoring setup guide (Prometheus + Grafana)
Backup and restore procedures
OTA update operational guide
Troubleshooting guide for common issues (OOM, thermal, model eviction)

items.title: Fix plugin manager lock scope and state atomicity
items.description: Module-level _STATE_LOCK shared across PluginManager instances. State persistence is non-atomic (read-modify-write). Move lock to instance level, use atomic file writes.
acceptance_criteria: Lock is per-instance, not module-level
State file writes use tmp+rename pattern (atomic)
Multiple PluginManager instances with different state files don't deadlock

items.title: Add OTA download signature verification (Ed25519)
items.description: OTA update downloads verified by SHA-256 checksum only. Ed25519 signature support exists but is optional. Make it required by default.
acceptance_criteria: Ed25519 signature verification required by default
Public key shipped with OS image or configurable via BMT_OTA_PUBKEY
Unsigned updates rejected unless BMT_OTA_ALLOW_UNSIGNED=true
Signature verification tested in unit tests

items.title: Add session/conversation persistence with SQLite
items.description: BMT AI OS chat is stateless — no conversation history stored. Add a sessions table (SQLite) with session_key, model, token counts, timestamps. Store all chat messages with session_id foreign key. Each /v1/chat/completions request optionally binds to a session.
acceptance_criteria: SQLite sessions table with session_key, model, created_at, updated_at, total_tokens
SQLite messages table with session_id, role, content, tokens, timestamp
Optional session_id parameter on /v1/chat/completions for multi-turn binding
Session auto-created on first message if not specified
GET /api/v1/sessions — list sessions with metadata
GET /api/v1/sessions/{id}/messages — retrieve conversation history

items.title: Build context engine with token budget and compaction
items.description: No context window management exists. Build a pluggable context engine that assembles messages within the model's token budget, auto-summarizes old turns when budget is exceeded, and maintains conversation coherence across long sessions. Inspired by OpenClaw's context engine pattern: bootstrap → ingest → assemble → compact → afterTurn.
acceptance_criteria: ContextEngine interface: assemble(), compact(), ingest()
Token counting before sending to LLM (tiktoken or model-specific)
Auto-compaction: summarize oldest turns when budget exceeded
Compaction checkpoints persisted in session metadata
Configurable token budget per model (default: 80% of context window)

items.title: Integrate RAG auto-injection into chat completions
items.description: RAG and chat are disconnected endpoints. When a user sends a chat message, automatically query the RAG pipeline for relevant context and inject retrieved chunks into the system prompt before calling the LLM. Configurable per-session: auto, manual, or disabled.
acceptance_criteria: Chat completions auto-query RAG when rag_mode=auto (default)
Retrieved chunks injected as system context with source attribution
RAG mode configurable per request: auto, manual, disabled
Source citations included in response metadata
RAG query uses last user message as search query

items.title: Add hybrid BM25 + vector memory search
items.description: Current RAG uses ChromaDB vector search only. Add BM25 keyword search (SQLite FTS5) and merge with vector results using weighted scoring and temporal decay. Inspired by OpenClaw's hybrid search: merged_score = vector_weight * vector_score + text_weight * bm25_score, with exponential temporal decay.
acceptance_criteria: SQLite FTS5 index for ingested documents alongside ChromaDB
Hybrid merge: configurable vector_weight (0.5) + text_weight (0.3)
Temporal decay: score *= exp(-ln(2)/half_life * age_days), default half_life=14
Fallback to FTS-only when embeddings unavailable
Search API: GET /api/v1/memory/search?q=...

items.title: Add conversation history API (list, search, export)
items.description: No way to retrieve, search, or export past conversations. Add API endpoints for conversation management: list with pagination, full-text search across messages, export to JSON/Markdown.
acceptance_criteria: GET /api/v1/sessions — paginated list with filters (date, model, token count)
GET /api/v1/sessions/{id} — full session metadata
GET /api/v1/sessions/search?q=... — full-text search across message content
GET /api/v1/sessions/{id}/export?format=json|markdown — conversation export
DELETE /api/v1/sessions/{id} — delete conversation and messages

items.title: Implement memory dreaming/consolidation system
items.description: Implement a background process that promotes frequently-recalled RAG snippets into durable MEMORY.md files. Uses a 6-component weighted scoring algorithm (frequency, relevance, diversity, recency, consolidation, conceptual) inspired by OpenClaw's dreaming system.
acceptance_criteria: Short-term recall tracker: records which RAG chunks are returned per query
Promotion scoring: 6-component weighted score (frequency 0.24, relevance 0.30, diversity 0.15, recency 0.15, consolidation 0.10, conceptual 0.06)
Configurable promotion thresholds: min_score=0.75, min_recall_count=3
Cron-scheduled consolidation (default: daily at 2 AM)
Promoted facts written to MEMORY.md with timestamps

items.title: Add multi-channel message delivery
items.description: Currently the controller only serves API responses. Add a channel system that can deliver AI assistant responses to external messaging platforms (webhooks, MQTT, WebSocket push). Initial channels: webhook and MQTT for IoT device fleet.
acceptance_criteria: Channel plugin interface: deliver(message, channel_config) -> DeliveryResult
Webhook channel: POST response to configurable URL
MQTT channel: publish to configurable topic (for IoT fleet)
Per-session channel binding: responses delivered to configured channels
Channel configuration via /api/v1/channels endpoints

items.title: Add SSE streaming to dashboard chat
items.description: Chat page waits for full response. Implement Server-Sent Events streaming: parse EventSource from /v1/chat/completions with stream=true, render tokens incrementally, show typing indicator during generation.
acceptance_criteria: Chat responses stream token-by-token in the UI
Typing indicator (animated dots) during generation
Streaming can be aborted mid-response with Stop button
Fallback to non-streaming if SSE connection fails

items.title: Add session persistence to dashboard chat
items.description: Chat history is lost on page refresh (React state only). Connect to the session/conversation API (BMTOS-69) to persist messages. Add session list sidebar, session switching, and auto-load history on revisit.
acceptance_criteria: Messages persist across page refreshes
Session list sidebar shows previous conversations
Click session to load its history
New Session button creates fresh conversation
Delete session with confirmation

items.title: Add context usage meter to chat interface
items.description: No visibility into token budget consumption. Add a context meter bar showing percentage of model context window used, with color gradient: green (<70%), amber (70-85%), red (>85%). Display token counts per message.
acceptance_criteria: Context meter bar above message area showing % used
Color gradient: green → amber (70%) → red (85%)
Hover shows exact token counts (used / total)
Per-message token badge (input/output tokens)

items.title: Add JWT auth integration to dashboard
items.description: Dashboard has no authentication. Add login page, JWT token storage in httpOnly cookie or localStorage, protected route middleware, and user display in sidebar header.
acceptance_criteria: Login page at /login with username/password form
JWT stored securely (httpOnly cookie preferred)
All pages except /login require authentication
Redirect to /login on 401 response
User role displayed in sidebar header
Logout button that revokes token

items.title: Add RAG toggle and source display to chat
items.description: RAG is disconnected from chat in the dashboard. Add a toggle button to enable/disable RAG auto-injection per conversation. When enabled, show retrieved source documents with attribution below the assistant response.
acceptance_criteria: RAG toggle button in chat header (on/off per session)
When on: relevant documents auto-retrieved and injected
Source attribution cards below assistant response
Source cards show: document name, relevance score, snippet
Click source card to expand full context

items.title: Add message grouping and improved chat styling
items.description: Current chat uses flat message list. Implement OpenClaw-style message grouping: consecutive messages from same role grouped with avatar, timestamps between groups, improved bubble styling with code block rendering.
acceptance_criteria: Consecutive same-role messages grouped under single avatar
Timestamps shown between message groups (relative: '2 min ago')
Code blocks rendered with syntax highlighting
Markdown rendering in assistant messages
Copy button on code blocks

items.title: Add slash commands to chat input
items.description: No command shortcuts in chat. Add slash command support with autocomplete menu: /clear (reset), /model (switch model), /rag (toggle RAG), /export (download conversation), /system (set system prompt).
acceptance_criteria: Typing / shows autocomplete dropdown with available commands
Arrow keys navigate, Enter selects, Escape dismisses
Commands: /clear, /model, /rag, /export, /system
/model shows model picker inline
/export downloads conversation as Markdown

items.title: Add file attachment and voice input to chat
items.description: Chat input only supports text. Add drag-drop file attachment (for RAG ingestion or image analysis) and voice input via Web Speech API. Show attachment preview thumbnails in compose area.
acceptance_criteria: Drag-drop files onto chat input area
Paste images from clipboard
Attachment thumbnail preview before sending
Microphone button for voice-to-text input
Voice input uses Web Speech API (browser native)

items.title: Implement LoRA/QLoRA fine-tuning training module
items.description: Create bmt_ai_os/training/ module with LoRA fine-tuning pipeline using PyTorch + PEFT. Support dataset loading, training loop with SummaryWriter, checkpoint saving, and progress reporting via API.
acceptance_criteria: LoRA fine-tune a 1.5B model on Jetson/ARM64
QLoRA support with <2GB VRAM
Training writes TensorBoard logs to /var/lib/bmt/runs/
Progress reported via /api/v1/training/jobs endpoint
Configurable hyperparameters (lr, epochs, rank, alpha)

items.title: Add training CLI commands (train, data-prepare, export)
items.description: Add CLI subcommands: bmt-ai-os train lora, bmt-ai-os data-prepare, bmt-ai-os export-model. Wire to the training module.
acceptance_criteria: bmt-ai-os train lora --model X --data Y launches training
bmt-ai-os data-prepare converts datasets to training format
bmt-ai-os export-model converts adapter to GGUF for Ollama

items.title: Add training job management API endpoints
items.description: Create /api/v1/training/ routes: POST /jobs (start), GET /jobs (list), GET /jobs/{id}/status (progress), DELETE /jobs/{id} (cancel). Background job execution with status tracking.
acceptance_criteria: POST /api/v1/training/jobs starts a training job
GET /api/v1/training/jobs lists all jobs with status
GET /api/v1/training/jobs/{id}/status returns progress %
Training runs in background thread/process

items.title: Wire TensorBoard to training pipeline with SummaryWriter
items.description: Integrate torch.utils.tensorboard.SummaryWriter into the training loop. Log loss, eval_loss, learning_rate, GPU memory, throughput (tok/s) to the training_runs volume.
acceptance_criteria: Training writes TensorBoard event files to /runs/{job_id}/
Metrics logged: loss, eval_loss, lr, memory, tok/s
TensorBoard at :6006 shows live training curves
Logs persist across container restarts (named volume)

items.title: Add training dashboard page with job status and TensorBoard embed
items.description: Create /training dashboard page showing: active/completed jobs, progress bars, loss curves (inline chart or TensorBoard iframe), start new training form.
acceptance_criteria: Dashboard /training page lists training jobs
Shows progress %, estimated time remaining
Inline loss curve chart or TensorBoard iframe
Start new training form with model/data selector

items.title: Build data preparation and dataset tools
items.description: Create bmt_ai_os/training/data_prep.py with tools to convert text, JSONL, CSV into training format. Support instruction tuning format (alpaca, sharegpt).
acceptance_criteria: Convert JSONL/CSV to instruction-tuning format
Support alpaca and sharegpt formats
Validation: check for empty/malformed examples
CLI: bmt-ai-os data-prepare --input X --format alpaca

items.title: Add model export pipeline (adapter merge + GGUF conversion)
items.description: After LoRA training, merge adapter weights back into base model and convert to GGUF format for Ollama. Auto-register the exported model.
acceptance_criteria: Merge LoRA adapter into base model weights
Convert merged model to GGUF via llama.cpp
Create Ollama Modelfile and register model
CLI: bmt-ai-os export-model --adapter X --output Y

items.title: Add training metrics to Prometheus and Grafana
items.description: Expose training metrics (loss, throughput, GPU memory) as Prometheus metrics. Add training panel to Grafana dashboard.
acceptance_criteria: Training metrics exposed on /metrics endpoint
Grafana dashboard has training panel
Alerting on training failure or stalled jobs

items.title: Create training documentation (guides + API reference)
items.description: Write docs/training/ with: overview, LoRA guide, data preparation guide, model export guide, TensorBoard usage.
acceptance_criteria: docs/training/overview.md exists
docs/training/lora-guide.md with step-by-step
docs/training/data-preparation.md
docs/training/model-export.md

items.title: Add MCP server for Claude Code native integration
items.description: Implement Model Context Protocol server so Claude Code can natively access BMT AI OS resources (files, RAG, models) without going through the OpenAI-compat API.
acceptance_criteria: MCP server exposes file system resources
MCP server exposes RAG query tool
MCP server exposes model management tools
Claude Code can connect via mcp:// protocol

items.title: Add tool_use/function_calling to OpenAI-compatible API
items.description: Extend /v1/chat/completions to support tools/function_calling parameters. Route to providers that support it (Anthropic, OpenAI). Required for advanced Claude Code features.
acceptance_criteria: tools parameter accepted in chat completions request
Tool calls returned in response when provider supports it
Anthropic provider maps tools to Claude format
Graceful fallback for providers without tool support

items.title: Add persona system with SOUL.md and IDENTITY.md workspace files
items.description: Implement OpenClaw-style persona via markdown files. SOUL.md defines personality/tone, IDENTITY.md defines name/role/avatar. Files loaded from agent workspace directory, assembled into system prompt with priority ordering.
acceptance_criteria: SOUL.md file defines assistant personality and tone
IDENTITY.md file defines name, role, avatar metadata
Files loaded from /data/bmt_ai_os/agents/{id}/ workspace
Priority ordering: agents(10) → soul(20) → identity(30) → user(40) → tools(50) → memory(70)
Budget constraints: 20K chars per file, 150K total

items.title: Auto-inject persona system prompt in OpenAI-compatible API
items.description: When no system message is provided by the client, auto-inject the agent's assembled persona as the first system message. Configurable via BMT_DEFAULT_PERSONA env var.
acceptance_criteria: Auto-inject persona when client sends no system message
Skip injection when client provides their own system message
BMT_DEFAULT_PERSONA env var selects persona workspace
RAG prompts use persona-aware template instead of hardcoded

items.title: Add persona editor to dashboard settings page
items.description: Dashboard UI for editing SOUL.md and IDENTITY.md content. Includes preset persona library (coding assistant, general helper, creative writer) and live preview.
acceptance_criteria: Settings page section for persona configuration
Textarea editor for SOUL.md content
Preset personas: coding assistant, general helper, creative writer
Save persona to agent workspace directory
Live preview of system prompt assembly

items.title: Support multi-agent personas with isolated workspaces
items.description: Each agent gets its own workspace directory with its own SOUL.md. Route messages to agents by session key or API parameter. Different agents can have completely different personalities.
acceptance_criteria: Multiple agents defined in controller config
Each agent has isolated workspace with own SOUL.md
Agent selection via agent_id parameter in chat API
Per-agent model and thinking level defaults

items.title: Add persona presets library (coding, general, creative)
items.description: Ship default SOUL.md templates for common use cases. Include coding assistant (precise, code-focused), general helper (friendly, balanced), and creative writer (expressive, imaginative).
acceptance_criteria: 3 preset SOUL.md templates shipped with the OS
Coding assistant: precise, technical, cites docs
General helper: friendly, balanced, conversational
Creative writer: expressive, imaginative, storytelling
CLI command to apply preset: bmt-ai-os persona set <preset>

items.title: Wire SSE streaming into chat-interface.tsx
items.description: Import streamChat from api.ts, add AbortController, parse SSE chunks, render tokens incrementally, add Stop button and typing indicator.

items.title: Wire session sidebar into chat page
items.description: Import SessionSidebar, add to chat layout, connect session load/save/switch/delete to chat state, auto-save on every message.

items.title: Wire slash commands into chat input
items.description: Import SlashCommandMenu and commands, detect / prefix in input, show/hide menu, execute commands (clear, model, rag, export, system).

items.title: Wire voice input and file drop into chat
items.description: Import VoiceInput and FileDropZone, wrap textarea, handle file attachments and voice transcription, show previews.

items.title: Wire RAG toggle and source cards into chat
items.description: Import SourceCard, add RAG toggle switch to chat header, call queryRag before sendChat when enabled, display sources below assistant messages.

items.title: Create persona editor component for settings
items.description: Build PersonaEditor with editor/presets/preview tabs. Add persona API functions to api.ts. Render in settings page.

items.title: Add persona CLI commands (set/get/list)
items.description: Add persona group to cli.py with set/get/list subcommands. Set copies preset SOUL.md to workspace.

items.title: Add /agents page for multi-agent management
items.description: Create agents dashboard page listing configured agent personas, their workspaces, and default models. Allow switching active agent.

items.title: Wire auth headers into all dashboard API calls
items.description: Ensure apiFetch includes Authorization Bearer header from stored JWT. Handle 401 redirect to /login on all pages.

items.title: Workspace 3-panel resizable layout
items.description: Resizable 3-panel layout (left sidebar, center content, right context panel) with CSS Grid + ResizeObserver. Left: agent/session browser. Center: tabbed work area. Right: RAG sources/memory/tools.

items.title: Tabbed work area with split view
items.description: Tab system for multiple chat sessions, code files, docs. Drag-drop reordering, close buttons, split view (horizontal/vertical). Active tab persistence in localStorage.

items.title: RAG knowledge base manager
items.description: Visual collection browser at /knowledge. Ingest files/folders with progress bar. Search across collections. View ingested document chunks. Delete collections.

items.title: WebSocket notification center
items.description: Real-time notifications via WebSocket. Toast component for alerts. Training job completion, fleet status changes, OTA updates. Bell icon in header with unread count.

items.title: Terminal emulator (xterm.js)
items.description: WebSocket-based terminal in browser using xterm.js. Run CLI commands on the device. Split terminal panes. New WebSocket endpoint in controller.

items.title: Code editor (Monaco)
items.description: Monaco editor integration for in-browser code editing. AI-assisted inline completions via provider. File save to device filesystem. Syntax highlighting.

items.title: File manager with tree view
items.description: Browse device filesystem. Upload/download files. Drag files into chat for context. File preview (code, markdown, images). New /api/v1/files endpoint.

items.title: Training studio with live metrics
items.description: Visual training pipeline builder. Live TensorBoard embedding (iframe). Dataset preview and validation. Real-time training metrics via WebSocket.

items.title: Fleet dashboard with device grid
items.description: Grid view of all fleet devices with health status. Per-device monitoring cards. Remote model deployment button. Fleet-wide command broadcast.

items.title: Theme toggle and layout persistence
items.description: Dark/light theme toggle with next-themes. Save panel sizes and layout per user in localStorage. Collapsible panels with memory.

items.title: Provider CRUD API (register/update/delete)
items.description: Add POST/PUT/DELETE /api/v1/providers endpoints. Persist config to providers.yml. Dynamic provider registration without restart.

items.title: Add Provider form in dashboard
items.description: Modal form: provider type dropdown, base URL, API key (masked), default model, Test Connection. Calls POST /api/v1/providers.

items.title: Per-provider edit/delete/toggle UI
items.description: Each provider card gets Edit (inline form), Enable/Disable toggle, Delete (with confirm). Updates via PUT/DELETE API.

items.title: Test Connection button
items.description: Button in Add/Edit form that calls provider health_check and shows result. Green check or red X with error message.

items.title: Fallback chain drag-drop configurator
items.description: Visual fallback chain editor. Drag-drop to reorder provider priority. Shows: provider1 → provider2 → provider3. Saves order to config.

items.title: Provider model list per provider
items.description: Expandable section in provider card showing models available from that provider. Calls list_models on the provider.

items.title: SSH WebSocket proxy backend with paramiko
items.description: New controller/ssh_ws.py: WebSocket endpoint /ws/ssh that accepts host, port, username, key_id params. Uses paramiko to establish SSH, pipes stdin/stdout between WebSocket and SSH channel. Supports resize, keepalive, and graceful disconnect.

items.title: Terminal page SSH mode with connection form
items.description: Update /terminal page: add connection form (host, port, username, auth method). Toggle between Local (PTY) and SSH modes. SSH mode connects to /ws/ssh with params. Show connection status indicator.

items.title: Fleet device Connect button opens SSH terminal
items.description: Add 'SSH' button to each fleet device card. Click opens /terminal?host={ip}&user=root pre-filled. Auto-connects if device is online.

items.title: SSH key management API and UI
items.description: CRUD API for SSH keys: POST /api/v1/ssh-keys (upload), GET (list), DELETE. Keys stored encrypted in SQLite. UI in settings page to manage keys.

items.title: Multi-tab terminal with split panes
items.description: Multiple terminal tabs (like workspace tabs). Each tab is an independent xterm instance. Split horizontal/vertical. Tab persistence in localStorage.

items.title: Guided provider setup wizard (multi-step)
items.description: Multi-step wizard: 1) Select provider type 2) Choose auth method (API key/OAuth) 3) Enter credentials 4) Test connection 5) Select default model. Stepper UI with back/next.

items.title: Rich model catalog with cost and context info
items.description: Unified model catalog showing: model name, provider, context window size, max tokens, cost per 1M tokens (input/output), quantization. Sortable/filterable table on a /models/catalog page.

items.title: Provider health dashboard with expiry tracking
items.description: Enhanced provider cards: credential freshness indicator, API key last-used timestamp, error count with cooldown timer, response latency trend (last 10 checks), auto-refresh every 30s.

items.title: Multi-credential profiles per provider
items.description: Support multiple API keys per provider for load balancing. Round-robin or failover selection. Per-profile usage stats. Cooldown on rate-limit errors.

items.title: Provider auto-discovery (scan local ports)
items.description: Scan common local ports (11434 Ollama, 8001 vLLM, 8002 llama.cpp) on startup. Auto-register discovered providers. Show 'Discovered' badge in dashboard.

items.title: Per-persona workspace directories and RAG collections
items.description: Each persona (coding, general, creative, custom) gets its own workspace directory under workspace/agents/<persona_name>/ with subdirs for notes/, files/, and SOUL.md. Backend: extend persona_routes to return workspace path, auto-create dirs on persona switch, scope RAG ingest/query to persona collection. Directory structure: workspace/agents/coding/, workspace/agents/general/, workspace/agents/creative/, etc.
acceptance_criteria: GET /api/v1/persona/presets returns workspace_path per persona
workspace/agents/<name>/ auto-created with SOUL.md, notes/, files/ subdirs
RAG ingest scopes to persona-named ChromaDB collection
RAG query accepts collection param to scope results
Switching persona updates active workspace path in session
Tests: unit test for workspace creation and collection scoping

items.title: Obsidian-compatible markdown parser (wiki-links, frontmatter, tags)
items.description: Add markdown processing that understands Obsidian format: parse [[wiki-links]] into navigable links, extract YAML frontmatter metadata, support #tags inline, handle ![[embeds]] for images/notes. Backend: new chunker strategy for Obsidian vault files that preserves link graph. Frontend: render wiki-links as clickable navigation.
acceptance_criteria: [[wiki-link]] syntax parsed into navigable links in rendered markdown
YAML frontmatter extracted and displayed as metadata
#tag syntax extracted and displayed as badges
![[embed]] syntax renders referenced note/image inline
RAG chunker preserves wiki-link references in chunk metadata
Tests: parser correctly handles nested links, missing targets, edge cases

items.title: Persona-scoped Knowledge & Files tab in dashboard
items.description: Update the Knowledge page to show files/collections filtered by active persona. Add persona selector dropdown. File browser defaults to persona workspace. RAG search scopes to persona collection. Collections tab shows only persona collections.
acceptance_criteria: Knowledge page shows active persona name in header
Persona selector dropdown switches workspace and collection
Files tab defaults to workspace/agents/<active>/files/
Collections tab shows only active persona collection
Search tab scopes queries to active persona collection
Switching persona refreshes all tabs

items.title: Obsidian vault note editor with live preview
items.description: Add a markdown note editor to the Knowledge page that supports Obsidian features: split view (edit + preview), wiki-link autocomplete from vault files, frontmatter form, tag input, backlinks panel showing which notes link to current note.
acceptance_criteria: Split view: markdown source (left) and rendered preview (right)
Wiki-link autocomplete suggests existing vault files on [[ trigger
Frontmatter form editor for title, tags, date fields
Backlinks panel shows list of notes that link to current note
New note button creates file in workspace/agents/<name>/notes/
Ctrl+S saves and auto-ingests into persona RAG collection

items.title: Auto-ingest persona vault into RAG on file changes
items.description: When files are created/edited/deleted in a persona workspace, automatically re-ingest affected files into the persona RAG collection. Backend: file watcher or post-write hook in file_routes that triggers ingest for the owning persona. Debounce to avoid excessive re-indexing.
acceptance_criteria: File write/create in persona workspace triggers re-ingest of that file
File delete removes corresponding chunks from persona collection
Debounce: max 1 ingest per file per 5 seconds
Ingest runs async (does not block file save response)
Logs ingest activity with file path and chunk count

items.title: Chat RAG context auto-scoped to active persona
items.description: When chatting, the RAG query automatically uses the active persona collection instead of default. System prompt includes persona SOUL.md + relevant vault context. If no persona-specific results, fall back to default collection.
acceptance_criteria: Chat completion auto-queries active persona RAG collection
System prompt includes active persona SOUL.md content
RAG results from persona collection injected as context
Falls back to shared/ collection if persona collection has no results
No persona active: uses default collection as before

items.title: Vault graph view and backlinks panel
items.description: Build a simple graph visualization showing connections between notes via wiki-links (like Obsidian graph view). Display as an interactive canvas with nodes (notes) and edges (links). Add a backlinks panel in the note editor showing incoming links. Use a lightweight graph library or CSS/SVG-based rendering.
acceptance_criteria: Graph canvas renders notes as nodes, wiki-links as edges
Nodes are clickable to navigate to note
Graph auto-layouts with force-directed algorithm
Backlinks panel in note editor shows incoming link count + list
Graph filters: by tag, by date range, by connection depth
Works with 100+ notes without lag

items.title: Embedded terminal panel in Code Editor (bottom split)
items.description: Add a resizable terminal panel at the bottom of the Code Editor page, like VS Code. Toggle with a button or Ctrl+`. Uses the existing useTerminal hook and WebSocket /ws/terminal endpoint. Panel is collapsible with drag handle. Shares the editor flex layout: FileTree (left) | Editor+Terminal (right, vertical split).
acceptance_criteria: Terminal panel visible at bottom of Code Editor page
Toggle with Ctrl+backtick keyboard shortcut
Uses existing useTerminal hook and /ws/terminal WebSocket
Shares flex layout with editor: vertical split
Terminal panel has its own status bar (connected/disconnected)
Panel hidden by default, toggle button in editor toolbar

items.title: tmux session management via WebSocket
items.description: Backend: modify terminal_ws.py to spawn tmux instead of plain /bin/sh. If tmux is available, run "tmux new-session -A -s bmt" so the session persists across reconnects. Add BMT_TERMINAL_CMD env var (default: auto-detect tmux, fallback to sh). Frontend: show session name in status bar, add "New Tab" button that creates tmux windows.
acceptance_criteria: Backend spawns tmux new-session -A -s bmt instead of plain sh
Falls back to /bin/sh if tmux not available
tmux session persists across WebSocket reconnects
BMT_TERMINAL_CMD env var overrides default command
Frontend shows session name in terminal status bar
Tests: unit test for tmux detection and fallback

items.title: Terminal auto-connects on editor page load
items.description: When the Code Editor page opens, automatically connect the embedded terminal to the local WebSocket (no click required). Reconnect on disconnect. Terminal starts in the current workspace directory (cd to workspace path). Show connection status dot in the terminal toolbar.
acceptance_criteria: Terminal connects automatically when editor page loads
No click required to establish connection
Auto-reconnects on disconnect (max 3 attempts, exponential backoff)
Terminal starts in workspace directory (cd workspace)
Connection status dot visible in terminal toolbar

items.title: Terminal panel resize with drag handle
items.description: Add a draggable resize handle between editor and terminal panels. Persist panel height in localStorage. Double-click handle to toggle terminal open/closed. Minimum height 100px, maximum 60% of container.
acceptance_criteria: Drag handle between editor and terminal panels
Drag to resize: min 100px, max 60% of container
Double-click handle toggles terminal open/closed
Panel height persisted in localStorage
Resize triggers xterm.js fit() to adjust columns/rows

items.title: Workspace directory structure convention and auto-scaffold
items.description:
Define and auto-create the standard workspace layout on first boot or workspace init. Backend: GET /api/v1/settings/workspace returns the structure, POST /api/v1/workspace/init creates it. Directory convention:

workspace/
├── agents/                  # Per-persona isolated environments
│   ├── coding/
│   │   ├── SOUL.md          # Persona definition
│   │   ├── notes/           # Obsidian vault (markdown knowledge base)
│   │   ├── files/           # Working files for this persona
│   │   └── .chroma/         # Persona-scoped vector collection name
│   ├── general/
│   └── creative/
├── projects/                # User project directories
│   └── <project-name>/     # Cloned repos, code projects
├── data/                    # Datasets, training data, exports
│   ├── datasets/            # Training datasets (JSONL, CSV, Parquet)
│   ├── models/              # Fine-tuned model exports (GGUF, LoRA adapters)
│   └── exports/             # Benchmark reports, conversation exports
├── uploads/                 # File upload landing zone
├── shared/                  # Shared knowledge across all personas
│   ├── notes/               # Global Obsidian vault
│   └── documents/           # Reference documents for RAG
└── .config/                 # Workspace config
    ├── workspace.json       # Workspace metadata (created_at, version)
    └── preferences.json     # User preferences (theme, defaults)

Auto-scaffold on first access. Dashboard Settings shows the layout. File browser and editor default to workspace root. Each persona auto-creates its subdirs on first switch.
acceptance_criteria: GET /api/v1/workspace/structure returns full directory tree
POST /api/v1/workspace/init creates all standard directories
Auto-scaffold on first GET /api/v1/settings/workspace if dirs missing
Creates: agents/, projects/, data/{datasets,models,exports}, uploads/, shared/{notes,documents}, .config/
workspace.json created with version, created_at metadata
Settings page shows workspace layout with descriptions
Tests: unit test for scaffold idempotency

items.title: Multi-provider AI coding integration (Claude, Codex, Gemini)
items.description: Extend the Code Editor AI panel to support multiple AI providers beyond the default Ollama. Add provider selector (Claude/Anthropic, OpenAI/Codex, Gemini, local Ollama). Each provider uses existing provider registry. Route AI requests through the selected provider.
acceptance_criteria: AI panel shows provider selector (Claude, OpenAI, Gemini, Ollama)
Each provider lists its available models
Selected provider+model persisted in localStorage
Streaming works with all providers via /v1/chat/completions
Provider with no API key shows setup prompt

items.title: Enhanced Model Manager with provider catalog and probing
items.description: Clone OpenClaw model catalog pattern: rich model cards with context window, pricing, capabilities. Add model probe (test connectivity with simple prompt). Group models by provider. Show model status (available/unavailable).
acceptance_criteria: Model catalog shows cards with context window, pricing tier, capabilities
Models grouped by provider (Local, Cloud)
Probe button tests model connectivity
Model status: available (green), unavailable (red), untested (gray)
Search/filter by provider, capability, name

items.title: Provider API key management in Code Editor
items.description: Quick-setup flow when user selects a provider without API key. Inline key input in the AI panel. Link to Settings for full key management. Show key status (valid/invalid/missing) per provider.
acceptance_criteria: Provider without key shows inline setup prompt
API key input saves to provider config API
Key validation on save (test with probe)
Link to Settings page for advanced key management

items.title: Model comparison and A/B prompt testing
items.description: Send the same prompt to 2+ models side by side. Show responses in split columns. Compare speed, token count, output quality. Useful for evaluating which model works best for a coding task.
acceptance_criteria: Compare button sends prompt to 2 selected models simultaneously
Split view shows both responses side by side
Shows latency, token count per response
User can apply either response to editor

items.title: Diff preview before applying AI-generated code
items.description: Show a unified diff view between current file content and AI-generated code before applying. User reviews changes (green=added, red=removed), then clicks Apply or Reject. Uses a simple side-by-side or inline diff renderer.

items.title: Slash commands in AI prompt (/fix, /refactor, /explain, /test)
items.description: Add slash command support in the AI prompt textarea. Typing / shows a command palette with coding actions. Each command pre-fills a specialized system prompt. Commands: /fix (fix bugs), /refactor (improve code), /explain (explain code), /test (generate tests), /doc (add documentation), /optimize (performance).

items.title: AI tool use: read files, run commands, search code
items.description: Enable the AI to use tools during code generation. Backend: extend the chat endpoint to support function calling with tools: read_file, write_file, run_command, search_code. Frontend: show tool invocations inline in the response (collapsible tool call blocks).

items.title: Multi-file edit workflow from AI prompt
items.description: AI can suggest edits across multiple files from a single prompt. Response shows a file tree of changes with per-file diffs. User can apply all or select individual files. Uses structured output format with file paths and content.

items.title: Git integration in Code Editor (status, diff, commit)
items.description: Add git panel to the editor: show file status (modified/staged/untracked), view diffs, stage files, commit with message. Backend: add git endpoints for status, diff, stage, commit. Uses the workspace directory as git root.
